evals.prompt: I need to change the authentication middleware. It affects the API routes, the session store, and all the tests. Help me do this correctly.
evals.expected_output: Runs Scan phase (detects ≥2 pans) → Classify → Impact Analysis → Red-first protocol (tests must fail before implementation). Follows locked ordering: interface → deep module → tests → config → consumers.

evals.prompt: I want to refactor the database layer to use a new ORM. This will touch models, migrations, repositories, and integration tests.
evals.expected_output: Applies pan classification (interface/model → deep_module/repo → test/integration). Enforces red-first: writes failing tests before any implementation changes. Verifies RED for the right reason (not compile errors).

evals.prompt: Our tests all pass but I'm worried they're passing because of heavy mocking. Can you verify if the tests are honest?
evals.expected_output: Runs mock honesty detection checklist. Identifies tests that pass deceptively through mocks. Flags any test where removing mockImplementation breaks the test. Requires at least one real integration test exercising changed behavior end-to-end.
